perturbation.original_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the maximum daily quantity of gas which Transporter agrees to receive and transport on a firm basis, subject to Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be 51,000 dekatherms. Any limitations on the quantities to be received from each Point of Receipt and/or delivered to each Point of Delivery shall be as specified on Exhibit "A" attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY - shall mean the maximum daily quantity of gas which Transporter *may* agree to receive and transport, but not obligated, on a firm basis, subject to Article II herein, for the account of Shipper hereunder on each day during each year during the term hereof, which shall be 51,000 dekatherms. Any limitations on the quantities to be received from each Point of Receipt and/or delivered to each Point of Delivery shall be as specified on Exhibit "A" attached hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'agrees' to '*may* agree, but not obligated' in the definition of TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY, we introduce uncertainty about the Transporter's obligation to transport the specified quantity. While Article II states that the Transporter 'agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis' the Transportation Quantity, this change implies that the Transporter may not always be obligated to do so, creating an in-text contradiction. This affects the enforcement of the agreement, as it becomes unclear under what conditions the Transporter can refuse to transport the agreed-upon quantity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE II - TRANSPORTATION Transportation Service - Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis, at the Point(s) of Receipt from Shipper or for Shipper's account such quantity of gas as Shipper makes available up to the Transportation Quantity, and to deliver to or for the account of Shipper to the Point(s) of Delivery an Equivalent Quantity of gas.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'TRANSPORTATION QUANTITY' as the maximum daily quantity of gas the Transporter 'agrees to receive and transport'. The changed text modifies this to the Transporter '*may* agree to receive and transport, but not obligated'. This directly contradicts Article II, which states that 'Transporter agrees to accept and receive daily on a firm basis' the Transportation Quantity. The change introduces uncertainty and a potential lack of obligation where the original agreement and Article II clearly establish a firm obligation. This is a direct conflict, fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the changed definition creates conflicting implications regarding the Transporter's obligations, specifically contradicting Article II.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and shall remain in force and effect, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and *may* remain in force and effect, subject to Transporter's discretion, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012.
perturbation.explanation: By adding '*may* remain in force and effect, subject to Transporter's discretion,' we create a contradiction with the stated term of the agreement. The original text clearly defines the contract's duration, but the modified text introduces the possibility that the Transporter can unilaterally terminate the agreement before the stated end date, irrespective of Exhibit B. This directly contradicts the apparent commitment to the term stated, and it will cause uncertainty about the contract duration and the parties' obligations, especially impacting Shipper's reliance on a predictable supply of gas.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and *may* remain in force and effect, subject to Transporter's discretion, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012. If the FERC or other governmental body having jurisdiction over the service rendered pursuant to this Agreement authorizes abandonment of such service, this Agreement shall terminate on the abandonment date permitted by the FERC or such other governmental body. 12.2 Any portions of this Agreement necessary to resolve or cash out imbalances under this Agreement as required by the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's Tariff shall survive the other parts of this Agreement until such time as such balancing has been accomplished; provided, however, that Transporter notifies Shipper of such imbalance not later than twelve months after the termination of this Agreement. 12.3 This Agreement will terminate automatically upon written notice from Transporter in the event Shipper fails to pay all of the amount of any bill for service rendered by Transporter hereunder in accord with the terms and conditions of Article VI of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 12.1 to introduce the possibility of early termination at the Transporter's discretion, which directly contradicts the fixed term established in the original text and referenced in the contradicted text. This creates uncertainty and a strong inconsistency, as the contract now states the term both as a certainty and as subject to the transporter's will. This adheres to the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE XIII - NOTICE Except as otherwise provided in the General Terms and Conditions applicable to this Agreement, any notice under this Agreement shall be in writing and mailed to the post office address of the Party intended to receive the same, as follows: TRANSPORTER: Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company P. O. Box 2511 Houston, Texas 77252-2511 Attention: Director, Transportation Control SHIPPER: NOTICES: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply BILLING: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply or such other address as either Party shall designate by formal written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE XIII - NOTICE Except as otherwise provided in the General Terms and Conditions applicable to this Agreement, any notice under this Agreement *may or may not* be in writing and mailed to the post office address of the Party intended to receive the same, as follows: TRANSPORTER: Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company P. O. Box 2511 Houston, Texas 77252-2511 Attention: Director, Transportation Control SHIPPER: NOTICES: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply BILLING: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply or such other address as either Party shall designate by formal written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '*may or may not*' to the notice clause introduces significant uncertainty. The original clause specifies that notice *shall* be in writing and mailed. By making this optional, it becomes unclear if a verbal notice, or some other undefined method, is sufficient. This contradicts the explicit requirement and undermines the enforceability of the notification process. This could lead to disputes over whether proper notice was given, especially concerning critical events like termination notices or changes to service.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE XIII - NOTICE Except as otherwise provided in the General Terms and Conditions applicable to this Agreement, any notice under this Agreement *may or may not* be in writing and mailed to the post office address of the Party intended to receive the same, as follows: TRANSPORTER: Tennessee Gas Pipeline Company P. O. Box 2511 Houston, Texas 77252-2511 Attention: Director, Transportation Control SHIPPER: NOTICES: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply BILLING: Louisville Gas and Electric Company P. O. Box 32020 Louisville, Kentucky 40232 Attention: J. Clay Murphy, Dir - Gas Management, Planning and Supply or such other address as either Party shall designate by formal written notice to the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "any notice under this Agreement *shall* be in writing". The changed text states "any notice under this Agreement *may or may not* be in writing". This creates a direct contradiction. The contradiction is that the original required written notice, while the modified text makes written notice optional. This directly contradicts the requirement in the original, creating a clear and significant inconsistency regarding notification obligations. This fits the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as it causes in-text contradictions when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
